title: cruciata laevipes subsp. laevipes
intro: Cruciata laevipes subsp. laevipes, also known as the green alder or water germander, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and stems, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The health benefits associated with Cruciata laevipes subsp. laevipes include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to relieve colds, coughs, and respiratory problems.
